stories.title: HMRC Stakeholder Digest: 2 July 2026
stories.summary: HMRC published its latest stakeholder digest including a call for evidence on the design, operation and future direction of voluntary National Insurance Contributions (NICs). The digest also covers key Corporation Tax updates.

stories.title: Nantwich Man First to Be Fined for Sending Art to Russia
stories.summary: Jonathan Hornby from Nantwich, Cheshire, became the first person fined by HMRC for attempting to send artwork to Russia in breach of UK sanctions, receiving a total fine of £30,085. The case marks an enforcement milestone for HMRC's sanctions compliance work.

stories.title: Business Tax Briefing – 3 July 2026: Electricity Generator Levy Rate Rise
stories.summary: The Electricity Generator Levy (EGL) rate has been increased from 45% to 55% with effect from 1 July 2026, as covered in Deloitte's latest Business Tax Briefing. This affects generators of electricity from low-carbon and renewable sources.

stories.title: Government Response to Phase 1 of the Southport Inquiry Published
stories.summary: The Home Office has published the government's formal response to Phase 1 of the Southport Inquiry, announcing work with policing to develop a new model as outlined in the 'From local to national' White Paper. The response also covers recommendations relating to online safety legislation.

stories.title: Government Extends Pub Licensing Hours for England World Cup Match
stories.summary: The Home Office and MHCLG extended licensing hours until 5am to allow football fans across England to watch the World Cup match, drawing widespread national and regional coverage. The decision reflects government flexibility on licensing for major sporting events.

stories.title: FCDO Services Annual Report and Accounts 2025–2026 Published
stories.summary: FCDO Services, the executive agency and trading fund owned by the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, has published its Annual Report and Accounts for 2025 to 2026. The report sets out financial performance and operational delivery for the year.

stories.title: UK ISAR Team Continues Venezuela Earthquake Response
stories.summary: Scottish firefighters remain deployed in Venezuela as part of the UK's International Search and Rescue (ISAR) response, supporting ongoing operations following a major earthquake. The FCDO is coordinating the UK's disaster relief effort.

stories.title: Keep Britain Working: Six-Month Progress Update Published
stories.summary: DWP has published the June 2026 progress update for 'Keep Britain Working', the initiative driving a fundamental rebalancing in how health and disability are supported in the workplace. The update outlines progress and sets the direction for the forthcoming Vanguard Phase.

stories.title: BAE Systems Tops DWP's 2026 Apprenticeships Employer Rankings
stories.summary: BAE Systems has topped the DWP's newly published apprenticeship employer leaderboard for 2026, which ranks leading employers by the quality and scale of their apprenticeship programmes. The rankings are designed to promote best practice across UK businesses.

stories.title: New Digital Government Information Sharing Regulations Laid Before Parliament
stories.summary: DWP and DSIT have jointly laid new Digital Government (Disclosure of Information) Regulations 2026 before Parliament, expanding data sharing powers under the Digital Economy Act 2017 to support passported benefits and reduce fuel poverty.

stories.title: Air Pollution Awareness Coalition (APAC) Launched
stories.summary: DEFRA has published the terms of reference for a new cross-sector Air Pollution Awareness Coalition (APAC), aimed at improving public understanding of air pollution and supporting action to reduce harm. DEFRA will provide overall co-ordination and secretariat support.

stories.title: DEFRA Chewing Gum Task Force Grants New Council Funding
stories.summary: A local council has received new funding through DEFRA's Chewing Gum Task Force grant scheme, run in partnership with environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy. The scheme tackles the environmental and financial burden of chewing gum litter on public spaces.

stories.title: £4.6 Billion Contract Secured for Next-Generation Fighter Jet with Italy and Japan
stories.summary: The UK Ministry of Defence has secured a £4.6 billion trilateral contract with Italy and Japan to deliver the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP) next-generation combat aircraft. This is a major milestone in the programme to field a cutting-edge stealth fighter by the mid-2030s.

stories.title: WWI Durham Light Infantry Soldier Laid to Rest on 110th Anniversary of the Somme
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence and Veterans UK oversaw a ceremony on the Somme (30 June 2026) to lay to rest a World War One soldier from the Durham Light Infantry, on the eve of the 110th anniversary of the Battle of the Somme. The service honoured one of many soldiers killed during that historic campaign.

stories.title: Salisbury Plain Wildfire Must Burn Out Naturally Due to Unexploded Ordnance
stories.summary: A wildfire on Salisbury Plain military training ground is being allowed to burn out on its own, as unexploded shells in the area make it too dangerous for firefighters to tackle directly. Military training has sparked hundreds of wildfires across UK countryside since 2023.

stories.title: UK-India Free Trade Deal to Enter Into Force on 15 July 2026
stories.summary: The landmark UK-India Free Trade Agreement, agreed in June 2026, is set to enter into force on 15 July 2026. DBT-led trade negotiations concluded a deal that could significantly boost UK GDP, lowering tariffs on British goods including whisky and automobiles.

stories.title: Visa Fees Reimbursement Scheme for Scale-Up Companies Launched
stories.summary: DBT has published the privacy notice for the new Visa Fees Reimbursement Scheme for Scale-Ups (VFRS4SU), designed to improve the UK's international competitiveness by helping fast-growing companies attract global talent. The scheme reimburses visa costs for eligible scale-up businesses.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: UK Government Plans to Ban Under-16s from Social Media
stories.summary: The UK government, led by DSIT, has announced plans to ban under-16s from social media platforms as part of a wider digital safety push. The government has also launched an AI Growth Lab for the legal sector and published a major AI standards strategy.

stories.title: New Digital Government Information Sharing Regulations Laid Before Parliament
stories.summary: DSIT has laid the Digital Government (Disclosure of Information) Regulations 2026 before Parliament, expanding powers under the Digital Economy Act 2017. The regulations expand data sharing to support passported benefits and reduce fuel poverty across the UK.

stories.title: Government Office for Technology Transfer (GOTT) Theory of Change Review Published
stories.summary: DSIT has published a progress evaluation and theory of change review for the Government Office for Technology Transfer (GOTT), established in 2022 to improve commercialisation of publicly funded research. The review assesses GOTT's impact and future direction.

stories.title: NHS to Overhaul Patient Communications and End 'Farce' of Late Appointment Letters
stories.summary: NHS England announced a major overhaul of patient communications, aiming to end the situation where appointment invitations arrive after the appointment itself. The reforms will modernise how patients are contacted about their care.

stories.title: Patients Awaiting Hospital Treatment to Get Three Weeks' Notice
stories.summary: Under new NHS England plans, patients on waiting lists will receive at least three weeks' notice before their hospital appointment, giving them sufficient time to prepare. The Guardian reports this as a key reform to improve patient experience.

stories.title: Royal Mail Scraps Saturday Second-Class Letter Delivery Nationwide
stories.summary: Royal Mail has begun rolling out its new delivery model across the UK in 2026, scrapping Saturday second-class letter deliveries while maintaining parcel deliveries up to seven days a week. Leaflets have been sent to households explaining the changes.

stories.title: Royal Mail Warns It May Suspend Deliveries to Addresses with Dogs
stories.summary: Royal Mail has issued a warning to customers that deliveries may be suspended to properties where dogs pose a risk to postal workers, urging homeowners to put up signs. The policy is part of ongoing efforts to protect delivery staff safety.
